title: Tailwind compatibility
description: To use Tailwind with ACoreX, you need to integrate them. We’ve provided functions and classes that make development easier and faster for you.

sections.title: Install Tailwindcss
sections.description: ACoreX can work independently of TailwindCSS, but we recommend using it. To install and configure TailwindCSS, follow the steps below. Additionally, ACoreX provides custom classes tailored to the ACoreX design system through TailwindCSS.<u> ACoreX currently supports Tailwind 3, but it will soon migrate to version 4. </u>

sections.title: Configuration Tailwindcss
sections.description: To add the Tailwind config, first run the command to automatically generate the config file, then add the ACoreX preset to it.

sections.title: Predefined classes
sections.description: ACoreX provides a set of predefined classes that can be used to style your components. These classes are based on the ACoreX design system and are designed to be used with TailwindCSS.

rows: apply h1 font size, weight and line height
apply h2 font size, weight and line height
apply h3 font size, weight and line height
apply h4 font size, weight and line height
apply h5 font size, weight and line height
apply h6 font size, weight and line height
apply heading border bottom, line height and margin
apply subtitle font size, weight and line height
apply headings font size, weight and line height
apply headings text align start with border
apply headings text align center with border
apply headings text align end with border

sections.title: Surface classes
sections.description: ACoreX provides a set of surface classes that can be used to style your components. These classes are based on the ACoreX design system and are designed to be used with TailwindCSS.
